barRaise.topFinding: Twenty-third review, fourth straight healthy verdict. No commits have landed since the 06-15 release (f61c179), so the code is byte-for-byte the board from the last three reviews: zero HIGH, two AI-owned MEDIUMs that neither block 1.0, and four LOWs. The only working-tree movement is cosmetic and uncommitted (a CONTEXT.md size note in CLAUDE.md plus four comment-only stylesheet repoints in src/mobile-hooks*.ts). The one item that moved is observability, and it moved the wrong way a fourth time: today's clean 06-18 canonical audit landed but the dashboard's audit.latestReportPath in Chains.json still points at CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-14, now four audits stale (06-15/16/17/18 all clean on disk), up from three on 06-17. The gap widens by one every day the daily audit fires and no release does, because the dashboard refresh is welded to release time. It stays MEDIUM and benign only because all four skipped audits are clean. Decoupling that refresh is the highest-value AI-ownable fix left before 1.0.

actions.title: Decouple the dashboard `audit` block from release time: have the daily canonical-audit writer parse the high/medium/low counts from the latest `CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md` TL;DR and advance `audit.latestReportPath`, so a between-release HIGH cannot sit invisible. The pointer is now four audits stale (06-14 on the JSON, clean 06-15/06-16/06-17/06-18 on disk). This is the highest-value AI-ownable chore on the board.
Post-1.0, split the `worker/src/index.js` monolith into modules. No pre-1.0 action.
